'''Harry Bowes''' (born 7 September 2001) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|hk}} or {{rlp|lf}} for [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

==Playing career==

=== Junior Career ===
Bowes played his amateur rugby league for the [[Shaw Cross Sharks]]. He joined [[Wakefield Trinity]] at 15 years old, joining the scholarship side. 

===2020===
Bowes made his [[Super League]] debut for [[Wakefield Trinity]] against the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in round 11 of the [[Super League XXV|2020 Super League season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/54749063 |title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity 18-20 Leeds Rhinos |publisher=BBC Sport |date=1 November 2020}}</ref>

=== 2021 ===
He featured once for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the 2021 season, He joined [[Newcastle Thunder]] on a short-term loan, featuring in one game for the [[RFL League One]] side.<ref>{{Cite web |title=NEWCASTLE THUNDER CONFIRM NEW LOANS |url=https://www.thunderrugby.com/article/126/newcastle-thunder-confirm-new-loans |access-date=2025-06-16 |website=www.thunderrugby.com}}</ref> 

=== 2022 ===
In 2022 he played 11 games for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League XXVII]] season, finished 3rd from bottom. 

===2023===
Bowes played 13 games for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished bottom of the table and were relegated to the [[RFL Championship]] which ended their 24-year stay in the top flight.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/20876/12962152/super-league-wakefield-trinity-relegated-after-loss-at-leigh-leopards-st-helens-and-wigan-warriors-win/|title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity relegated after loss at Leigh Leopards, St Helens and Wigan Warriors win|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

On 8 November 2023, it was reported that he had signed for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=8 Nov 2023|title=Featherstone Rovers add Wakefield Trinity starlet to 2024 squad as they confirm Jack Bussey retention|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/featherstone-rovers-add-wakefield-trinity-starlet-harry-bowes-to-2024-squad-as-they-confirm-jack-bussey-retention}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-11-08 |title=Featherstone Rovers boss James Ford excited by 'explosive' young signings |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/featherstone-rovers-boss-james-ford-excited-by-explosive-young-signings-4401463 |access-date=2025-06-16 |website=Wakefield Express |language=en}}</ref>

===2024===
His debut for [[Featherstone Rovers]] came against Hunslet in first round of the 1895 Cup, coming off the bench in the 12-62 win. He went on to feature another 25 times, scoring a singular try. [[Featherstone Rovers]] finished 6th in the table, qualifiers for the eliminators. Bowes did not feature in the 25-12 loss against Bradford Bulls. 

On 19 Oct 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Keighley Cougars]] in the [[RFL League 1]] on a 1-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Keighley News|date=19 Oct 2024|accessdate=30 Oct 2024|author=Nathan Atkinson|title=Keighley Cougars sign hooker from Featherstone Rovers|url=https://www.keighleynews.co.uk/sport/24661922.keighley-cougars-sign-hooker-featherstone-rovers/}}</ref>

=== 2025 ===
His debut for [[Keighley Cougars]] came in the second round of the [[2025 Challenge Cup]], starting at hooker in the 12-72 loss against [[RFL Championship]] side [[York Knights]].

Starting at hooker against [[Dewsbury Rams]], Bowes got over for a try early on in the second half, but soon after, was left on the floor following a nasty collision. Reports from local paper, the [[Telegraph & Argus]], suggested that Bowes had suffered a seizure following that collision, with him eventually leaving the field following a lengthy period of treatment from both sides’ medical staff.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-04-05 |title=SQUAD: Cougars hit by Hatton blow as Kilshaw looks for debut win |url=https://www.keighleynews.co.uk/sport/25066018.lewis-hatton-not-part-keighley-cougars-squad-injury/ |access-date=2025-06-16 |website=Keighley News |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-03-31 |title=Former Wakefield Trinity youngster Harry Bowes given all clear after suffering seizure in League 1 match |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-wakefield-trinity-youngster-harry-bowes-given-all-clear-after-suffering-seizure-in-league-1-match |access-date=2025-06-16 |website=Love Rugby League |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-03-31 |title=Cougars lose again as afternoon marred by scary seizure for hooker |url=https://www.keighleynews.co.uk/news/25050296.keighley-cougars-lose-suffer-several-awful-injuries/ |access-date=2025-06-16 |website=Keighley News |language=en}}</ref>

On 8 August 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan until the end of the 2025 season<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=8 Aug 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Former Wakefield Trinity starlet makes Championship switch as League 1 loan exit confirmed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-wakefield-trinity-starlet-makes-championship-switch-as-league-1-loan-exit-confirmed}}</ref>

On 11 October 2025 it was reported that he had made his move to [[Sheffield Eagles]] permanent.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=11 Oct 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Former Wakefield Trinity young gun makes fresh Championship move ahead of 2026|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/harry-bowes-makes-sheffield-move-permanent-ahead-of-2026}}</ref>
